public void addRange(int l, int r) {
    // Find the first interval that could overlap
    // Start with the left neighbor
    Integer key = map.floorKey(l);
    if (key != null && map.get(key) > l) {
        // overlaps with left neighbor
        int newL = key;
        int newR = map.get(key);
        map.remove(key);
        l = newL;
        r = newR;
    }
    // Now check the right side
    while (true) {
        Map.Entry<Integer, Integer> nextEntry = map.ceilingEntry(r);
        if (nextEntry != null && nextEntry.getKey() < r) {
            // overlaps with nextEntry
            r = nextEntry.getValue();
            map.remove(nextEntry.getKey());
        } else {
            break;
        }
    }
    map.put(l, r);
}

public void addRange(int l, int r) {
    // Find left neighbor
    Integer leftKey = map.floorKey(l);
    if (leftKey != null && map.get(leftKey) > l) {
        // overlaps with leftKey
        l = leftKey;
        r = map.get(leftKey);
        map.remove(leftKey);
    }
    // Now check right neighbors
    while (true) {
        // Find the first interval whose start is >= l and <= r
        // Since TreeMap is sorted, the next entry after leftKey is the first candidate
        Integer nextKey = map.ceilingKey(l + 1); // or ceilingEntry(l)
        if (nextKey != null && nextKey <= r) {
            // this interval starts at nextKey which is <= r
            int nextEnd = map.get(nextKey);
            r = Math.max(r, nextEnd);
            map.remove(nextKey);
        } else {
            break;
        }
    }
    map.put(l, r);
}

public void removeRange(int l, int r) {
    List<Integer> toRemove = new ArrayList<>();
    List<Interval> toAdd = new ArrayList<>();

    for (Map.Entry<Integer, Integer> entry : map.entrySet()) {
        int start = entry.getKey();
        int end = entry.getValue();
        if (end > l && start < r) {
            // Overlap
            if (start < l) {
                toAdd.add(new Interval(start, l));
            }
            if (end > r) {
                toAdd.add(new Interval(r, end));
            }
            toRemove.add(start);
        }
    }

    for (int key : toRemove) {
        map.remove(key);
    }

    for (Interval interval : toAdd) {
        addRange(interval.start, interval.end);
    }
}

But this approach would require iterating through all entries, which is O(n) time.

public void removeRange(int l, int r) {
    // Find all intervals that overlap with [l, r)
    // Collect them in a list, then process
    List<Integer> toRemove = new ArrayList<>();
    // Find the first interval that starts before r

            // Split into parts

                // add [key, l)
                addRange(key, l);
            }
            if (end > r) {
                // add [r, end)
                addRange(r, end);
            }
            // Remove the original interval
            map.remove(key);
        }
        // Move to the next key
        key = map.lowerKey(key);
    }
}

public void removeRange(int l, int r) {
    List<Integer> toRemove = new ArrayList<>();
    NavigableMap<Integer, Integer> candidates = map.subMap(0, true, r, false);
    for (Map.Entry<Integer, Integer> entry : candidates.entrySet()) {
        int start = entry.getKey();
        int end = entry.getValue();
        if (end > l) {
            toRemove.add(start);
            // Split into parts
            if (start < l) {
                addRange(start, l);
            }
            if (end > r) {
                addRange(r, end);
            }

public void addRange(int l, int r) {
    // Find the first interval that could overlap
    // Start with the left neighbor
    Integer key = map.floorKey(l);
    if (key != null && map.get(key) > l) {
        // overlaps with left neighbor
        int newL = key;
        int newR = map.get(key);
        map.remove(key);
        l = newL;
        r = newR;
    }
    // Now check the right side
    while (true) {
        Map.Entry<Integer, Integer> nextEntry = map.ceilingEntry(r);
        if (nextEntry != null && nextEntry.getKey() < r) {
            // overlaps with nextEntry
            r = nextEntry.getValue();
            map.remove(nextEntry.getKey());
        } else {
            break;
        }
    }
    map.put(l, r);
}

public void removeRange(int l, int r) {
    List<Integer> toRemove = new ArrayList<>();
    List<Interval> toAdd = new ArrayList<>();

    for (Map.Entry<Integer, Integer> entry : map.entrySet()) {
        int start = entry.getKey();
        int end = entry.getValue();
        if (end > l && start < r) {
            // Overlap
            if (start < l) {
                toAdd.add(new Interval(start, l));
            }
            if (end > r) {
                toAdd.add(new Interval(r, end));
            }
            toRemove.add(start);
        }
    }

    for (int key : toRemove) {
        map.remove(key);
    }

    for (Interval interval : toAdd) {
        addRange(interval.start, interval.end);
    }
}

So, code steps for removeRange:

public void removeRange(int l, int r) {
    List<Integer> toRemove = new ArrayList<>();
    NavigableMap<Integer, Integer> candidates = map.subMap(0, true, r, false);
    for (Map.Entry<Integer, Integer> entry : candidates.entrySet()) {
        int start = entry.getKey();
        int end = entry.getValue();
        if (end > l) {
            toRemove.add(start);
            // Split into parts
            if (start < l) {
                addRange(start, l);
            }
            if (end > r) {
                addRange(r, end);
            }
        }
    }
    for (int key : toRemove) {
        map.remove(key);
    }
}
